title: Sean McCoy
wikitext:
[]: Sean McCoy             Quote Post by thewayneiac » Wed Feb 23, 2005 8:52 pm Eva Monkey asked over on the A.N. forum just who is this guy that ADV put on the EP. 20 commentary. (For some reason he doesn't seem to have asked the same question here.) Well I found him. HERE are some posts of his on Usenet. Be prepared to be bombarded by gibberish.

[]:             Quote Post by The Eva Monkey » Thu Feb 24, 2005 3:03 pm I hate gibberish, as well as nazis. And ESPECIALLY Nazi Jibberish.

[]:             Quote Post by Ironfoot » Tue Mar 01, 2005 2:42 am Very, very interesting indeed.

[]:             Quote Post by bjamieson » Wed Mar 02, 2005 3:35 pm Ahahahaha!! I remember this guy on the Eva ML. I remember a lengthy argument on the ML over Asuka being pregnant at the end of the film. Wow... does ADV have any standards? Out of sick curiousity I really want to hear what this cat had to say.

[]:             Quote Post by Reichu » Wed Mar 02, 2005 9:46 pm bjamieson wrote: I remember this guy on the Eva ML. I remember a lengthy argument on the ML over Asuka being pregnant at the end of the film.

[]:             Quote Post by AchtungAffen » Wed Mar 02, 2005 9:53 pm Reichu wrote: bjamieson wrote: I remember this guy on the Eva ML. I remember a lengthy argument on the ML over Asuka being pregnant at the end of the film.

[]:             Quote Post by bjamieson » Thu Mar 03, 2005 5:53 am Reichu wrote: How on Earth does one get such an idea?

[]:             Quote Post by Shin-seiki » Thu Mar 03, 2005 6:30 am As I like to point out whenever this absurd notion comes up, we have Asuka complaining about her period in #22, which is probably, at most, a month or so before 3I. By this point in the story she is completely fed up with and hostile to Shinji, which makes the idea of the two of them "doing it" at any point in the timeline covered by #23-24 unlikely, to put it charitably. Furthermore, morning sickness is a symptom of pregnancy that only starts to show up by 7-8 weeks. Do the math :roll: In any case, how clueless does one have to be to fail to pick up on the basic point that a big factor in Asuka's antipathy towards Shinji was the fact that, on those occasions when she did try to "get it on" with him (#09 and #15), he responded with indifference. For Asuka and Shinji to have "done the dirty deed" makes utter nonsense of a very significant plot element...

[]:             Quote Post by Mr. Tines » Thu Mar 03, 2005 1:00 pm Shin-seiki wrote: Furthermore, morning sickness is a symptom of pregnancy that only starts to show up by 7-8 weeks. Do the math :roll:

[]:             Quote Post by DatDude » Thu Mar 03, 2005 1:04 pm For the love of god. Anno made a wise crack at a con a few years ago about Asuka have morning sickness thats all. She didn't get pregnent from Shinji either from sex, which is NEVER EVEN IMPLEYED IN THE SHOW, or the hospital scene cuz none of it was shown to have goten on HER! I rank this crackpot theory up their with the " pen-pen is god BS" .

[]:             Quote Post by Reichu » Thu Mar 03, 2005 2:34 pm Mr. Tines wrote: Shinji: I've done terrible thing s to Asuka... my emphasis on the plural.

[]:             Quote Post by Mr. Tines » Thu Mar 03, 2005 2:41 pm Reichu wrote: Japanese tends not to specify plurals.

[]:             Quote Post by Carl Horn » Fri Mar 04, 2005 8:55 pm Whether Asuka is meant to be pregnant at the actual point in time at which the film ends may be speculation. However, the ending does appear to leave them as the last man and woman on earth, or, if you like, the first man and woman on the new earth. If you accept the idea that they are a new Adam and Eve, that suggests Asuka would become pregnant by Shinji eventually. For that reason I don't find it shocking at least to entertain the idea that she is already pregnant by him--perhaps through one of the possibilities of the Instrumentality that shows the two of them in an apparent lovers' quarrel (in fact, this is where Shinji first is shown trying to strangle her). I am not saying that I accept the theory, but neither do I find it outrageous or absurd on its face.

[]:             Quote Post by Reichu » Fri Mar 04, 2005 9:07 pm Carl Horn wrote: Whether Asuka is meant to be pregnant at the actual point in time at which the film ends may be speculation. However, the ending does appear to leave them as the last man and woman on earth, or, if you like, the first man and woman on the new earth. If you accept the idea that they are a new Adam and Eve, that suggests Asuka would become pregnant by Shinji eventually.

[]:             Quote Post by DatDude » Sat Mar 05, 2005 5:30 pm did we figue out who this guy is yet?

[]:             Quote Post by Ryo » Sat Mar 05, 2005 5:51 pm Reichu wrote: Assuming they haven't mastered the "rhythm" method of birth control. :P "Get the hell away from me, Shinji! I'm ovulating!"

[]:             Quote Post by Reichu » Sat Mar 05, 2005 6:13 pm Ryo wrote: Somehow I didn't find this too hard to imagine...

[]:             Quote Post by DatDude » Sat Mar 05, 2005 6:24 pm Reichu wrote: Ryo wrote: Somehow I didn't find this too hard to imagine...

[]:             Quote Post by Mr. Tines » Sun Mar 06, 2005 3:42 pm Reichu wrote: Ryo wrote: Somehow I didn't find this too hard to imagine...

[]:             Quote Post by Reichu » Sun Mar 06, 2005 8:53 pm Mr. Tines wrote: There's a reason for the old joke Q: "What do you call a woman who uses the rhythm method?" A: "A mother."

(For some reason he doesn't seem to have asked the same question here.) Well I found him. HERE are some posts of his on Usenet. Be prepared to be bombarded by gibberish.
